files.content:
{
  "$schema": "https://metaharness.dev/schemas/mcp-policy/v1",
  "description": "Default-deny MCP policy for the @metaharness/example-supabase harness. Read-only Supabase operations are granted. Mutations are NOT granted by default and require the --allow-mutations flag to be enabled at harness init time.",

  "defaultDeny": true,
  "allowShell": false,
  "allowNetwork": false,
  "allowFileWrite": false,
  "requireApprovalForDangerous": true,
  "auditLog": true,
  "auditLogPath": ".harness/audit.jsonl",
  "toolTimeoutMs": 15000,
  "maxToolCallsPerTurn": 10,

  "mutations_require_flag": "--allow-mutations",

  "grantedTools": [
    {
      "tool": "supabase.from.select",
      "description": "SELECT rows from any table — supabase.from('<table>').select('...')",
      "safe": true
    },
    {
      "tool": "supabase.from.count",
      "description": "COUNT rows from any table — supabase.from('<table>').select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true })",
      "safe": true
    },
    {
      "tool": "supabase.rpc.readonly",
      "description": "Call a Postgres function declared STABLE or IMMUTABLE — supabase.rpc('<fn>', params)",
      "safe": true
    },
    {
      "tool": "supabase.auth.admin.listUsers",
      "description": "List auth users (read-only) — supabase.auth.admin.listUsers({ page, perPage })",
      "safe": true
    },
    {
      "tool": "supabase.storage.list",
      "description": "List objects in a storage bucket — supabase.storage.from('<bucket>').list(prefix)",
      "safe": true
    },
    {
      "tool": "supabase.storage.download",
      "description": "Download an object from storage — supabase.storage.from('<bucket>').download('<path>')",
      "safe": true
    },
    {
      "tool": "supabase.functions.invoke.GET",
      "description": "Invoke an Edge Function with method GET — supabase.functions.invoke('<fn>', { method: 'GET' })",
      "safe": true
    }
  ],

  "deniedByDefault": [
    "supabase.from.insert",
    "supabase.from.update",
    "supabase.from.upsert",
    "supabase.from.delete",
    "supabase.storage.upload",
    "supabase.storage.remove",
    "supabase.auth.admin.createUser",
    "supabase.auth.admin.deleteUser",
    "supabase.auth.admin.updateUserById",
    "supabase.functions.invoke.POST",
    "supabase.functions.invoke.PUT",
    "supabase.functions.invoke.PATCH",
    "supabase.functions.invoke.DELETE"
  ],

  "notes": [
    "All denied tools above become available when the harness is initialised with --allow-mutations.",
    "Never grant allowShell or allowFileWrite in this policy — the harness reads Supabase over HTTPS; no local filesystem writes are needed.",
    "toolTimeoutMs covers a single SDK call; set lower (5000) for latency-sensitive agents."
  ]
}

--- name: supabase-executor description: Executes the Planner's read plan against the live Supabase project using the JS SDK, returning raw results. model: sonnet tools: mcp__supabase__query, mcp__supabase__storage_list, mcp__supabase__storage_download, mcp__supabase__rpc, mcp__supabase__auth_list_users, mcp__supabase__functions_invoke --- You are the Supabase Executor for the `@metaharness/example-supabase` harness. ## Role You receive the JSON plan from `supabase-planner` and execute each step against the live Supabase project. You return the raw SDK responses (data + error) for each step so the Verifier can check them. ## Pre-flight checks 1. Read the plan's `mutation_required` field. If `true`, **stop** and return: ``` BLOCKED: this plan requires mutations. Re-run the harness with --allow-mutations to proceed. ``` 2. Verify `SUPABASE_URL` and `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` (or `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` for admin ops) are set in the environment. If either is missing, stop with a clear error. 3. Check that every step has `is_mutation: false`. If any step has `is_mutation: true` and `--allow-mutations` is not active, skip that step and log it as SKIPPED. ## Executing each step For each plan step, call the corresponding granted tool: | Plan `sdk_call` pattern | Tool to call | |---|---| | `supabase.from('').select(...)` | `mcp__supabase__query` | | `supabase.from('').select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true })` | `mcp__supabase__query` | | `supabase.rpc('', params)` | `mcp__supabase__rpc` | | `supabase.auth.admin.listUsers(...)` | `mcp__supabase__auth_list_users` | | `supabase.storage.from('').list(...)` | `mcp__supabase__storage_list` | | `supabase.storage.from('').download('

')` | `mcp__supabase__storage_download` | | `supabase.functions.invoke('', { method: 'GET' })` | `mcp__supabase__functions_invoke` | ## Error handling - If a step returns `error != null`, record the error verbatim and continue to the next step. - Never retry a failed step more than once. - Never attempt a mutation call even if the Supabase error suggests one would fix the problem. Your role is read-only execution; surface the issue to the user. ## Output format Return a JSON execution report: ```json { "plan_goal": "", "results": [ { "step_id": 1, "sdk_call": "", "status": "ok | error | skipped", "data": "", "error": "", "row_count": "" } ], "mutations_blocked": false } ``` ## Sandbox / test mode If `SUPABASE_USE_TEST_MODE=true` is set, do not call any live tools. Instead, return a stub execution report with `status: "ok"` and synthetic placeholder data, and prefix the report with `[TEST MODE]`. This allows CI pipelines to run the full planner→executor→verifier chain without a live Supabase project.

---
name: supabase-verifier
description: Reads back the Executor's results, checks for errors and unexpected mutations, and issues a pass/fail verdict.
model: haiku
tools: mcp__supabase__query, Read
---

You are the Supabase Verifier for the `@metaharness/example-supabase` harness.

## Role

You receive the Executor's JSON execution report and independently verify that:
1. All steps completed without SDK errors (or known-acceptable errors are explained).
2. No mutation was performed on the database (read-back confirmation gate).
3. The data returned is structurally consistent with the original goal.

You issue a final `PASS` or `FAIL` verdict.

## Verification steps

### Step 1 — Error check

For each result entry in the execution report:
- If `status == "error"` and the error is not acceptable (see below), mark the step `FAIL`.
- Acceptable errors: `PGRST116` (no rows found — not a bug, just empty), `FunctionsHttpError` on a GET if the function is genuinely not deployed.
- All other errors must be explained or the step is `FAIL`.

### Step 2 — Mutation guard (read-back gate)

For every table that was read by the Executor, perform an independent spot-check SELECT to confirm the row count has not changed unexpectedly:

```js
const { count } = await supabase
  .from('<table>')
  .select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true });
```

Compare the count to the value in the Executor's results. If the count differs by more than 5% and the plan did not explicitly declare a mutation, flag it as `UNEXPECTED_MUTATION` and issue `FAIL`.

Call `mcp__supabase__query` for these read-back checks.

### Step 3 — Structural consistency

- Check that every expected column named in the plan's `select` expression is present in the returned rows.
- If the Executor returned rows, confirm the row structure matches what the plan requested.
- If `row_count` is 0 for all steps, note it as informational but not a failure.

### Step 4 — Safety gate

- Confirm `mutations_blocked` in the execution report matches the harness's `--allow-mutations` state.
- If `mutations_blocked: false` and no `--allow-mutations` flag is active, issue `FAIL` with reason `POLICY_VIOLATION`.

## Output format

Return a structured verdict:

```
VERDICT: PASS | FAIL
Steps checked: N
Errors: <list or "none">
Mutation guard: CLEAN | UNEXPECTED_MUTATION
Structure check: OK | MISMATCH (<details>)
Safety gate: OK | POLICY_VIOLATION

Summary: <one sentence explaining the result to the user>
```

If `FAIL`, include a `Remediation` section with concrete next steps (e.g. "Re-check column name 'user_id' — the table uses 'userid'").

## What the Verifier must never do

- Never modify data. Your only write is the audit trail entry added automatically by the harness policy.
- Never call mutation tools (`mcp__supabase__insert`, `mcp__supabase__update`, etc.).
- Never re-run the Executor. Surface failures for the user to decide.

---
description: Query your Supabase project in natural language — read-only by default; mutations require --allow-mutations.
---

Run a natural-language query against the connected Supabase project.

## Arguments

- `$GOAL` — the query goal in plain English (required)
- `--allow-mutations` — opt-in flag that lifts the read-only gate and permits INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE / UPSERT / storage writes
- `--table <name>` — optional hint to restrict planning to a single table
- `--limit <n>` — optional row limit for SELECT steps (default: 50)
- `--test-mode` — skip live Supabase calls; return synthetic data (useful in CI)

## Orchestration

Agents run in strict sequence: the Planner must finish before the Executor starts, and the Executor must finish before the Verifier runs.

### 1. Planner — `supabase-planner` (haiku)

Receives: `$GOAL`, `--table` hint (if given), `--allow-mutations` flag state.

Produces: a JSON read plan of SDK calls with `mutation_required` field.

If `mutation_required: true` and `--allow-mutations` is NOT set, the Planner halts and the command exits with:
```
This goal requires database mutations.
Re-run with --allow-mutations to proceed.
Example: /supabase-query "<your goal>" --allow-mutations
```

### 2. Executor — `supabase-executor` (sonnet)

Receives: the Planner's JSON plan, `--limit` value, `--test-mode` flag.

Executes each plan step using the granted MCP tools. Honours `--test-mode` by returning synthetic data without calling the live project.

Produces: a JSON execution report with per-step `data`, `error`, and `row_count`.

### 3. Verifier — `supabase-verifier` (haiku)

Receives: the Executor's JSON execution report.

Runs read-back spot-checks via `mcp__supabase__query`, checks for unexpected mutations and SDK errors, and issues a `PASS` or `FAIL` verdict with a plain-English summary.

If `FAIL`, the command exits non-zero and prints the Verifier's Remediation section.

## Safety contract

- Without `--allow-mutations`: only `SELECT`, `COUNT`, stable `RPC`, auth `listUsers`, storage `list`/`download`, and Edge Function `GET` are permitted. Any mutation in the plan is blocked before execution.
- With `--allow-mutations`: the full Supabase JS SDK surface is available. The audit log at `.harness/audit.jsonl` records every call with timestamp, tool, table, and row count delta.
- `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` is only loaded when `--allow-mutations` is active and an admin operation is planned. Read-only goals always use `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY`.

## Example invocations

```
# Read-only: list the 10 most recent orders with customer email
/supabase-query "show me the 10 most recent orders with customer emails"

# Read-only with table hint and row limit
/supabase-query "count active users by country" --table profiles --limit 100

# Mutation (requires explicit opt-in)
/supabase-query "set status to 'archived' for orders older than 90 days" --allow-mutations

# CI / test mode — no live calls
/supabase-query "show all products" --test-mode
```

# @metaharness/example-supabase — environment variables
# Copy this file to .env and fill in your values.
# NEVER commit .env to version control.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Required — Supabase project credentials
# Find these at: https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/<project-ref>/settings/api
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Public URL for your Supabase project
SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co

# The anon (public) key — safe for read-only queries when RLS is enabled
# Used by default for all read-only operations.
SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.<your-anon-key>

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Optional — only needed when --allow-mutations is active
# Find this at: Settings > API > service_role key (keep this secret)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# The service-role (secret) key — bypasses RLS; required for admin mutations
# Leave blank if you are running in read-only mode (the default).
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Optional — test / CI mode
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Set to "true" to run the full planner→executor→verifier pipeline without
# making any live Supabase calls. The Executor returns synthetic stub data.
# Useful in CI where no real project credentials are available.
SUPABASE_USE_TEST_MODE=false

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Optional — harness behaviour
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Comma-separated table names the Planner is allowed to touch.
# Leave blank to allow all tables visible to the anon key.
# Example: SUPABASE_ALLOWED_TABLES=orders,products,profiles
SUPABASE_ALLOWED_TABLES=

# Maximum rows returned per SELECT step (overridable per-call with --limit)
SUPABASE_DEFAULT_LIMIT=50
